findings.description: Path traversal: client-controlled session.filename is joined into UPLOAD_DIR in assemble() with no sanitization, so a filename like '../../etc/cron.d/x.png' escapes the upload dir and writes attacker-controlled bytes to an arbitrary path (CWE-22/CWE-434).

The extension whitelist is bypassable for path-traversal and is the only content gate: '../../x.png' passes the ext check, and a whitelisted extension does not validate MIME/magic bytes, so a polyglot/HTML file with a .png name can be stored and later served (CWE-434 stored-XSS / content-type confusion).

Path traversal via upload_id: upload_id is fully client-controlled and never validated, and is interpolated into the chunk path f'{upload_id}.{index}', so receive_chunk can write outside CHUNK_DIR (e.g. upload_id='../uploads/shell') (CWE-22).

Unbounded disk write / size limit never enforced during upload: receive_chunk writes arbitrary-length data with no per-chunk or aggregate cap, and MAX_FILE_MB/file_size_ok is never invoked before storage, allowing disk-exhaustion DoS (CWE-400/CWE-770).

Resource exhaustion: start() accepts arbitrary total_chunks (only <1 rejected) and there is no cap on concurrent _sessions, so a client can declare billions of chunks or open unbounded sessions to exhaust memory/file descriptors/disk (CWE-770).

Improper verification ordering: assemble() moves the file into the live UPLOAD_DIR and deletes the session (line 70) before verify_checksum() or file_size_ok() can run, and neither is called internally, so an unverified/oversized/malicious file is persisted in the served directory (CWE-345/CWE-494, TOCTOU).

Memory-exhaustion DoS in verify_checksum: f.read() loads the entire (up to 200MB+, effectively unbounded given the missing upload limit) file into memory instead of streaming the hash in fixed-size blocks (CWE-400).

Symlink-following on write: open(dest_path,'wb') and the chunk writes follow pre-existing symlinks at predictable, client-influenced paths, enabling arbitrary-file overwrite if an attacker can pre-plant a symlink in UPLOAD_DIR/CHUNK_DIR (CWE-59); use O_NOFOLLOW or per-upload random subdirectories.

No authorization/ownership model: any caller knowing or guessing a client-supplied upload_id can receive_chunk/assemble/abort another user's session (IDOR / session hijack, CWE-639); upload_id should be a server-generated unguessable token bound to the authenticated principal.

Arbitrary overwrite of legitimate uploads: dest_path is derived solely from the client filename with no collision handling, so a client can overwrite another user's previously stored file of the same name (CWE-73).

Checksum compared with non-constant-time '!=' (hmac.compare_digest preferred); low impact here because the expected digest is client-supplied rather than a server secret, but flagged for completeness (CWE-208).

Off-by-one: range(session.total_chunks - 1) omits the final chunk during assembly, silently truncating every multi-chunk upload; security-adjacent because the truncated file then bypasses integrity expectations (deferred to logic critic for detail).
